claim_text: The VMS Pisces-first zodiac ordering is most parsimoniously explained as the combined product of the entering-sign convention (which makes Pisces = March) and the More Veneto calendar style (which makes March the first month of the year). This combination is specific to Venetian-territory calendars within the 1404-1438 production window and rules out Milanese, Pavian, German Imperial, and most Tuscan calendrical contexts. Padua becomes a viable production context from 22 November 1405 onward, when it adopts the More Veneto under Venetian rule.

supporting_evidence.passage_reference: VMS zodiac sequence starting with Pisces at f70v2
Italian calendar styles excluding Milan, Pavia, Florence, Siena, Pisa from 1 March new year
German Imperial calendar used Christmas style (Weihnachtsstil), December 25 new year; no Southern German diocese used March-starting year in the 14th-15th century

falsifier: Evidence that the More Veneto inference is wrong (per the falsifier of vms-2026-0003), OR evidence that the entering-sign convention does not apply (per the falsifier of vms-2026-0002), OR demonstration that Pisces-first ordering existed in a tradition outside More Veneto territories without requiring either convention, would weaken or collapse this synthesis. The synthesis remains a constraint on context rather than a positive identification of place of production.
discussion_notes: Synthesis claim. The dependency chain (vms-2026-0001 -> 0002, 0003 -> 0004) demonstrates the ledger's dependency-graph feature: if any of the foundational claims is retracted, this synthesis is automatically flagged. The synthesis is a constraint, not a proof: it eliminates several candidate production geographies but does not uniquely identify Venice or Padua. Additional positive evidence (paleographic, codicological, lexical) would be required to move from constraint to identification.
